reasoning_summary: A near-pi Rabi pulse at mod_depth 1 produces a paired post-pulse readout suppression near 3.87-3.875 GHz.
A localized post-pulse readout drop near 3.905 GHz is consistent with a near-pi-pulse pODMR resonance.
A detuned 52 ns square-pulse model predicts a localized dip and the readout2 deficit near 3.905 GHz matches a weaker but present resonance feature.
Near-pi modulated pulse readout shows a repeatable reference-normalized fluorescence dip around 3.860 GHz.
A near-pi Rabimodulated pulse produces a repeated localized drop in the post-pulse readout near 3.88 GHz.
A localized post-pulse readout dip near 3.875 GHz is consistent with a near-pi modulated Rabi pulse resonance.
A center-localized normalized dip near 3.875 GHz has the expected pODMR sign, though its 9% size is below the ideal 22% pi-pulse estimate.
A near-pi pulse at mod_depth 1 produces repeatable negative signal/reference contrast near 3.895-3.915 GHz.
A near-pi 52 ns mod_depth 1 pulse produces a localized dip in the post-pulse readout near 3.900-3.905 GHz relative to the m_S=0 reference.
The active 52 ns mod_depth 1 Rabi pulse should produce a near-pi ODMR dip, and readout 2 shows a localized post-pulse depression near 3.905 GHz.
A localized post-pulse readout dip near 3.895 GHz matches the expected Rabi-pulse contrast magnitude.
A near-pi 52 ns pulse at mod_depth 1 predicts a negative PL dip, and both readouts show a coherent several-sigma endpoint drop consistent with a pODMR resonance.
A near-pi 52 ns pulse at mod_depth 1 produces a localized post-pulse readout suppression relative to the bright reference near 3.875 GHz.
The near-pi pulse sequence shows a localized depression of the post-microwave readout near 3.900-3.905 GHz relative to the m_S=0 reference.
The active 52 ns mod_depth 1 Rabi pulse should produce a large resonant dark-state drop, and readout 2 shows a matching dip near 3.905 GHz.
A localized post-Rabi readout dip relative to the bright reference matches the expected near-pi pODMR response.
The post-pulse readout shows a multi-point dip relative to the flat bright reference for an approximately pi-length Rabi pulse.
The active 52 ns, mod_depth 0.3 pulse predicts an about 2.3-count resonant dip, and the post-pulse readout shows a matching center-frequency dip near 3.875 GHz.
A localized post-pulse fluorescence dip near 3.895 GHz is consistent with a Rabi-driven pODMR resonance.
A near-pi modulated Rabi pulse produces a readout-2-only dip near 3.880 GHz consistent with pODMR contrast.
The post-pulse readout has a localized dip at 3.880 GHz in the expected channel, consistent with a reduced-depth pODMR resonance.
A localized post-Rabi readout depression near 3.895-3.900 GHz is consistent with a resonance for the 52 ns mod_depth 1 pulse.
The active 52 ns modulated Rabi readout at mod_depth 1 shows a small but structured frequency-dependent contrast feature consistent with pODMR.
